summarization.a: X-ray diffraction patterns of 300cLi-MnO2, 200cLi-MnO2, and 100cLi-MnO2 samples, showing the different orientations of Li1-xMn2O4.
summarization.b: X-ray diffraction patterns of 300cLi-MnO2, 200cLi-MnO2, and 100cLi-MnO2 samples, showing the Lithiated phase, Li1-xMn2O4 (400), and ß-MnO2 (200).

a.question: How does the intensity of the peaks change when the number of lithium cycles increases?
a.answer: As the number lithium cycles increases, higher peaks can be observed.

a.question: At which value can an impurity phase be observed?
How can it be possible to avoid the impurity phase?
a.answer: This can be achieved using either 100 or 300 cycles of lithium deposition, since this phase can be observed at 200 lithium cycles.

b.question: What is the difference between the two XRD diffractograms shown in the figures?
b.answer: °2Θχ represents the in-plane and °2Θ represent the out-of-plane (Θ-2Θ).
